meta.title: 6.2 Object and Blob Storage
meta.description: S3, GCS, Azure Blob. Storage classes, lifecycle policies, pre-signed URLs, and cost optimization.

blocks.prompt: Your app stores user-uploaded photos. You expect 10 million photos growing by 100K/day. Should you store them in a database, on a file server, or somewhere else? What about photos uploaded 2 years ago that nobody looks at?

blocks.content:
## Object storage fundamentals

Object storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) stores unstructured data as objects in flat namespaces (buckets). Each object has a key (path), data (bytes), and metadata.

**Key characteristics:**
- Virtually unlimited storage capacity
- 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability
- Pay per GB stored + per request
- HTTP API (PUT, GET, DELETE)
- No file system hierarchy (flat namespace, / in keys is cosmetic)

### Storage classes and cost optimization

| Class (S3) | Access | Cost/GB/mo | Use case |
|---|---|---|---|
| Standard | Instant | $0.023 | Frequently accessed |
| Intelligent-Tiering | Auto | $0.023-0.004 | Unpredictable access |
| Standard-IA | Instant (min 30 days) | $0.0125 | Infrequent, fast access needed |
| Glacier Instant | Instant (min 90 days) | $0.004 | Archive, rare access |
| Glacier Deep Archive | 12 hours | $0.00099 | Compliance, never accessed |

**Lifecycle policies:** Automatically transition objects between classes. Example: after 30 days → Standard-IA, after 90 days → Glacier, after 7 years → delete.

### Pre-signed URLs

Grant temporary access to private objects without exposing credentials.

```
GET https://bucket.s3.amazonaws.com/photo.jpg?X-Amz-Signature=...&X-Amz-Expires=3600
```

**Upload flow:** Client requests a pre-signed upload URL from your server → Client uploads directly to S3 → S3 notifies your server via event. Your server never handles the file bytes.

### Multipart upload

For files >100MB, upload in parallel parts. If one part fails, retry only that part. S3 assembles the parts server-side.

Object storage (S3) provides virtually unlimited, highly durable storage at low cost. Use storage classes and lifecycle policies to optimize costs. Pre-signed URLs enable direct client uploads without proxying through your servers.

blocks.prompt: Module 6.3 covers search infrastructure — how to make your data findable with Elasticsearch, inverted indexes, and relevance tuning.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the durability guarantee of Amazon S3?
reviewQuestions.encourageCorrect: That is right! 11 nines of durability means your data is essentially never lost.
reviewQuestions.encourageIncorrect: S3 provides 11 nines (99.999999999%) durability — an incredibly strong guarantee.

reviewQuestions.question: What are pre-signed URLs used for?
options: Allowing clients to upload directly to object storage without proxying through your server
reviewQuestions.explanation: Pre-signed URLs let clients upload directly to S3, bypassing your server.
reviewQuestions.encourageCorrect: Exactly! Pre-signed URLs are essential for efficient file uploads at scale.
reviewQuestions.encourageIncorrect: Pre-signed URLs enable direct client uploads to object storage, avoiding proxy through your server.
